title: Plan-before-fan-out bracket: gated planning node before breakdown
description:
Make planning a first-class, reviewable, gated node in the DAG, sequenced before the implementation fan-out. A breakable container C is bracketed by a planning node P (agent plan-quality gate) and a breakdown node B (deterministic coverage-preview gate via validate --scope), with the impl subgraph spliced into the C->B link as a spine (P > B > impl > C). Front-end counterpart to epic 94d26c42's done-transition coverage enforcement.

## Success Criteria
- [hard] Breakable container scaffolds to a C->B->P spine with correctly typed, reduced nodes and a plan-quality gate on P.
- [hard] Breakdown gate blocks when drafted children leave a [hard] criterion uncovered, evaluated transitively over the impl interior (exit 4).
- [hard] Coverage traversal is transitive by default; child-type-exclude excludes bracket nodes and halts descent at them.
- [hard] jit plan <existing> retrofits a container, moving upstream deps onto P.
- [hard] Demonstrated on SDD (epic) and research (goal, no epic) with no methodology strings in Rust.
- jit-breakdown and project-lead drive plan->review->breakdown->coverage->implement; steering suite regression-tests it.

Design: dev/active/planning-bracket-design.md
